In all cases, this data may then be read-out with a read head. The write and read heads are typically integrated within a single assembly. To achieve steadily increasing data storage densities (typically measured in bits/inch2), which are now achieving levels near 1012 bits/in2, the sizes of magnetic regions storing individual bits have been reduced to nm levels. To achieve these increasing data storage densities, the dimensions (widths) of data tracks are being steadily decreased and the track-to-t...track spacing (the distance between the centers of adjacent tracks) are being reduced, allowing for more tracks to be stored on the disk and increasing the areal data storage density. This trend of increasing areal density, combined with the reduction of disk form factors, has enabled the storage of more data on smaller and more portable storage devices. Detailed Description of the Invention The gauge assembly comprises several key components: - A magnetic float that rises or falls in response to fluid level changes within the storage tank. - A magnetic sensor placed outside the storage container, which detects the movement of the magnetic float and translates this into an electrical signal proportional to the liquid level. These two elements work together without any physical contact between them, eliminating the need for a seal or other penetrations through the tank wall. This non-intrusive design allows safe monitoring even with volatile liquids that could present a safety hazard if exposed to electronic sensors directly. 5. Advantages of the Invention The primary advantages of this invention over previous technologies include: - Safety: The absence of electrical components within the fluid itself makes it suitable for use in environments where flammable or explosive vapors may be present. - Accuracy and reliability: Magnetic sensing technology provides accurate readings even at low levels, without being affected by viscosity or coatings on surfaces. - Easy installation and maintenance: The non-intrusive design means there are no seals to wear out or leaks to repair. We give an alternate definition later better suited to our purposes. Let $f_{w}(n)$ denote the number of factors of length $n$ in $w$, also known as the *subword complexity* of $O(n)$. It is well-known that $f_{w}(n) = n+1$ when $w$ is a Sturmian word. On the other hand, the Coven-Hedlund theorem [@covenhedlund] states that $f_{w}(n)$ is either bounded or $f_{w}(n) \geq n+1$ for all $n$. In this sense, Sturmian words are extremal with respect to subword complexity. A growing number of concussion experts [2] (3), strength and conditioning trainers (4), and physical therapists [3], believe that strengthening the neck may reduce the risk of concussion, the theory being that stronger neck muscles will help cushion against and lessen the linear and rotational forces that cause concussion. The new study adds to a growing body of evidence on the benefits of neck strengthening as a way to reduce brain trauma from concussion and repetitive sub-concussive blows, most recently the results of a 2014 study (5) suggesting that overall neck strength is a statistically significant predictor of concussion, with the odds of concussion falling by 5% for every one pound increase in aggregate neck strength. The data in that study also showed that the quarter of the subject group of more than six thousand high school athletes playing boys' and girls' soccer, basketball, and lacrosse with the weakest necks suffered the greatest number of concussions, while the quartile with the strongest necks suffered the fewest. Glucocorticoids (GC) down-regulate IL-6 synthesis in different cell types, including osteoblasts. Given the fact that bone remodeling is a tightly controlled process, it is reasonable to think of auto-regulatory mechanisms in the bone microenvironment able to prevent excess IL-6 production. We have studied two human osteosarcoma cell lines (Saos-2 and MG-63) with different degrees of differentiation and different constitutive IL-6 production (3.4 +/- 0.2 (mean +/- SE) and 2,898 +/- 401 pg/10(6) cells, respectively). We measured the expression of glucocorticoid receptor (GR) in terms of specific binding sites after exposure of cells to different amounts of IL-6. Incubation for 20 hours with IL-6 at increasing concentrations up to 2,000 pg/ml yielded significant increase of GR binding sites in both cell lines. IL-6 was also able to revert the inhibitory effect of dexamethasone (1 microM) on IL-6 production in Saos-2 cells but not in MG-63 cells. These results suggest that IL-6 up-regulates GR expression in human osteoblast-like cells, which could represent a feedback mechanism to inhibit IL-6 production and thus prevent bone loss. Tractors, industrial equipment, everything else that rolls on rubber? Each specialized type of tire requires a specialized design for its specific purpose. Aircraft tires, for instance, have to be very robust and handle a great deal of weight and stress, but for only a short period of time. Aircraft tires are often filled with an inert gas such as nitrogen, for more stable inflation levels, and are designed with specializespecialized features to withstand the intense heat and pressure during takeoff and landing. For example, aircraft tires operate at extremely high pressures—often exceeding 200 psi—and are constructed with reinforced casings to handle the rapid acceleration and heavy loads during flights. Even though they only make contact with the runway for a short duration, their design is critical for safety and performance. Similarly, off-road tires for tractors or construction equipment are built with deep treads and rugged compounds to grip muddy, rocky, or uneven terrain, while also resisting cuts and punctures. In contrast, high-performance tires for sports cars prioritize precision handling, sticky rubber compounds, and minimal rolling resistance for speed and efficiency. Each of these tires requires tailored maintenance and expertise. At our shop, we understand these nuances and are equipped to service a wide range of tires—from everyday passenger vehicles to specialized machinery. Whether you need routine checks, inflation, or replacement, our team ensures your tires are optimized for safety and performance. Still unsure which tires suit your needs?

The primary goal in the emergency department is to identify any life-threatening causes (secondary headaches) while also considering common benign conditions. Key steps include: 1. History Taking: Detailed history focusing on headache characteristics—onset, duration, intensity, location—and associated symptoms. 2. Physical Examination: Neurological examination and assessment for signs of systemic illness or focal neurological deficits. 3. Risk Stratification: - Red flags that suggest a secondary cause (e.g., thunderclap headaches, new-onset headache in older adults, immunocompromised state). 4. Diagnostic Testing: - Imaging: CT scan without contrast is often the first-line imaging study to rule out intracranial hemorrhage. - Lumbar puncture if subarachnoid hemorrhage or meningitis are suspected. 5. Management: - Immediate treatment for life-threatening conditions (e.g., aneurysmal rupture, hypertensive emergency). - Symptomatic relief with analgesics and antiemetics as needed. 6. Discharge Planning: - Referral to a specialist if necessary. - Follow-up care instructions. Suchautomated synthesistypicallyrequires the designer to prepare atextual description of the circuit layout using a hardware description language (HDL) wihich is inputinto anelectronic design automation tool (EDA). The EDA includes a logic synthesizer that translates the HDL descriptionand outputs netlist data representing components and interconnections in the integrated circuit layout. Typically, the designer selects from various available components suchas standard cell components, macrocellcomponents or fieldprogrammable gatearray (FPGA)components tobe included inthe integrated circuit design. Standardcells are basiclogic gates such as inverters, AND gates, OR gates, NAND gatesand NOR gates that are fabricated in silicon. Typically,the designer uses an EDA with a library ofstandardcell descriptions and specifies interconnections between the standard cells. The logic synthesizerproduces netlist data that includes thestandard cellsinterconnected to form the integrated circuit layout. A macrocell issimilar to a standard cell except that it performs more complex functions suchas anadder ora multiplier and typically comprises multiple standard cells connected in series, in parallel, or both. An EDA with a library of available macrocells may be used by the designer in designing an integrated circuit layout, wherein thelogic synthesizerproduces netlist data for theselected macrocells interconnected to form the integrated circuitlayout. FPGAs are programmable logic blocks that can be interconnected to form complex functions such asadders and multipliers.An EDA with a library of available FPGAs may also be used by the designerin designingan integratedcircuitlayout, whereinthelogic synthesizer producesnetlist data for theselected FPGAs interconnected to formthe integratedcircuit layout.
